title: Week 1 - Outlook and Email
summary: Introduction to school email - composing professional emails CC/BCC subject lines and attachments.

tasks: Task 1: Compose a professional email to a teacher|Task 2: Practice CC and BCC|Task 3: Email etiquette quiz

summary: Cloud storage vs local storage - organising files sharing documents and version history.

tasks: Task 1: Upload a file to OneDrive|Task 2: Share a document|Task 3: Restore a previous version

success_criteria: I can enter data into a spreadsheet|I can write a SUM formula|I can create a basic chart

title: Week 1 - What is a Digital System?
summary: Introduction to digital systems - the four key components and how hardware and software work together.
definitions: Digital system: Hardware and software working together to process information|Hardware: Physical components you can touch|Software: Programs and instructions you cannot touch|Input: Data going into a system|Output: Result coming out of a system|Processing: The CPU doing work on data|Storage: Saving data for later use
success_criteria: I can define what a digital system is|I can identify the four key components|I can give examples of input and output devices
tasks: Task 1: Label a digital system diagram|Task 2: Sort devices into input/output/processing/storage|Task 3: Explain how a system works in your own words

title: Byte's World - Digital Systems Story
summary: Follow Byte the file on an adventure through a digital system covering hardware software and file journeys across Weeks 1-3.
definitions: Input: Data going INTO the computer|Process: The CPU doing work on the data|Storage: Saving data - RAM or SSD|Output: The result coming out|CPU: The brain of the computer|RAM: Fast memory - only while power is on|SSD: Saves data permanently|Hardware: Physical parts you can touch|Software: Programs and instructions|Upload: Sending a file OUT to the cloud|Download: Getting a file IN from the cloud|Network: Devices connected to share data|Cloud: A building full of servers far away
success_criteria: I can explain what a digital system is|I can trace a file journey from creation to cloud|I can identify hardware and software components
tasks: Week 1 - Digital systems intro|Week 2 - Hardware and software|Week 3 - Byte's big journey
key_concepts: digital systems, hardware, software, file journey, cloud

title: Week 2 - Hardware and Software Deep Dive
summary: CPU RAM HDD vs SSD operating systems application software vs system software and performance factors.
definitions: CPU: Central Processing Unit - the brain of the computer|RAM: Random Access Memory - fast temporary storage|HDD: Hard Disk Drive - slower permanent storage|SSD: Solid State Drive - fast permanent storage|Operating system: Software that manages hardware (Windows macOS iOS)|Application software: Programs users run (Word Chrome)|System software: Software that runs the computer|GHz: Gigahertz - measure of CPU speed
success_criteria: I can explain the role of the CPU and RAM|I can compare HDD and SSD storage|I can identify examples of operating systems
tasks: Task 1: Compare HDD vs SSD specs|Task 2: Identify software types|Task 3: Performance factors quiz
key_concepts: CPU, RAM, storage, operating system, performance

tasks: Quiz - Week 2 Digital Systems

title: Week 3 - File Journeys through Digital Systems
summary: How files travel through networks - LAN WAN protocols IP addresses compression and file types.
definitions: Network: Connected devices that share data|LAN: Local Area Network - devices in one building|WAN: Wide Area Network - devices across cities or countries|Internet: A global WAN|Protocol: Rules for how data is sent|HTTP/HTTPS: Rules for sending web data (S = secure)|IP address: Unique number identifying a device|Packet: A small chunk of data sent across a network|Compression: Making a file smaller|Lossy: Compression that removes some data permanently|Lossless: Compression that keeps all data
success_criteria: I can trace a file journey from input to output|I can explain the difference between LAN and WAN|I can describe what packets are
tasks: Task 1: Trace a file journey diagram|Task 2: Sort protocols into categories|Task 3: Explain compression in your own words
key_concepts: networks, LAN, WAN, protocols, file journey

title: Week 4 - Binary Numbers Introduction
summary: Why computers use binary - bits bytes place values and converting between binary and decimal.
definitions: Binary: A number system using only 0s and 1s|Bit: A single binary digit - 0 or 1|Byte: 8 bits grouped together|Denary: Our normal base-10 number system|Place value: The value of a digit based on its position|128 64 32 16 8 4 2 1: The 8-bit place values
success_criteria: I can explain why computers use binary|I can convert binary to decimal|I can convert decimal to binary
tasks: Task 1: Fill in the place value table|Task 2: Convert binary to decimal|Task 3: Convert decimal to binary
key_concepts: binary, bits, bytes, conversion, place values

title: Week 6 - Binary Images
summary: How images are stored digitally - pixels resolution colour depth file size calculations.
definitions: Pixel: A single tiny dot that makes up a screen image|Resolution: The number of pixels in an image|Colour depth: Number of bits used to represent each pixel|1-bit colour: Black and white only|8-bit colour: 256 possible colours|24-bit colour: Over 16 million colours|File size: width x height x colour depth divided by 8 = bytes|Bitmap: Image made of pixels|Vector: Image made of mathematical shapes
success_criteria: I can explain what a pixel is|I can calculate image file size|I can explain the effect of colour depth on file size
tasks: Task 1: Calculate file sizes for different resolutions|Task 2: Compare 1-bit 8-bit and 24-bit images|Task 3: Bitmap vs vector comparison

title: Week 6 Quiz - Binary Images
summary: Short quiz on pixels resolution and colour depth.
tasks: Quiz - Week 6 Binary Images

title: Week 7 - Binary Sound
summary: How sound is stored digitally - analogue vs digital sampling sample rate bit depth and file size.
definitions: Analogue: Continuous signal like a real sound wave|Digital: Data stored as discrete numbers|Sampling: Taking snapshots of a sound wave|Sample rate: How many samples per second (Hz)|Bit depth: How many bits per sample|44100 Hz: CD quality sample rate|WAV: Uncompressed audio format|MP3: Compressed audio format (lossy)|File size: sample rate x bit depth x duration x channels / 8
success_criteria: I can explain the difference between analogue and digital|I can describe what sampling means|I can calculate audio file size
tasks: Task 1: Diagram an analogue to digital conversion|Task 2: Calculate audio file sizes|Task 3: Compare WAV and MP3
